themes.hook: India is currently walking a tightrope where one misstep in the Strait of Hormuz costs the RBI exactly 20 basis points in inflation.
key_data_points: A 10% increase in global crude prices raises Indian headline inflation by ~20 basis points.
~39% of global oil supply passes through the Strait of Hormuz daily.
India is potentially absorbing $3B worth of Russian crude currently stranded at sea.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'Hormuz Exception'—where Iran reportedly allows non-US/Israel ships to pass—isn't just a diplomatic win; it's a massive hidden subsidy for Indian OMCs. While global prices spike on 'risk,' India's ability to navigate the blockade creates a massive arbitrage opportunity that keeps the fiscal deficit from exploding.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, do you think the market is underestimating the 'diplomatic premium' India enjoys in energy procurement, or is the OMC margin squeeze by the government a bigger risk?

source_articles.title: RBI July 2025 Bulletin: Crude and Inflation
Iran allowing non-US/Israel ships through Hormuz
HSBC Slams Brakes on Indian OMCs

themes.hook: The 'Ozempic' of India is coming, and it won't cost $1,000. It will likely cost less than your monthly broadband bill—and that's a problem for pharma margins.
key_data_points: India’s weight-loss drug market is projected to surge by ₹5,000 Cr by 2030.
The cost of developing a New Chemical Entity (NCE) has hit $1 billion.
Patent expiry for Ozempic (Semaglutide) is set to trigger a generic boom in India.
themes.non_obvious_angle: Indian pharma is so efficient at commoditization that they destroy the 'innovation premium' almost instantly. The moment Semaglutide goes generic, it becomes a 'volume-only' game where only the firms with the most aggressive supply chains survive, making the $1B R&D hurdle for domestic innovation look even more daunting.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, given the rapid commoditization of complex generics, should Indian pharma investors pivot toward hospital chains or stick with the manufacturers?

source_articles.title: Ozempic patent expiry set to trigger weight-loss drug boom in India
India’s Weight-Loss Drug Market Set for ₹5,000 Cr Surge
Semaglutide generic is going to be super crowded market

themes.hook: 2024 is the year of record IPOs and remarkably mediocre returns. We’ve turned the primary market into a dumping ground for private equity exits.
key_data_points: Record number of IPOs in the Indian primary market with 'muted' post-listing returns.
Nomura has slashed the Nifty target to 24,900 by December 2026.
RBI is tightening Capital Market Exposure directions for banks to prevent overheating.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The real risk isn't a market crash—it's 'valuation indigestion.' Retail investors are so focused on the F&O debate that they aren't noticing that the 'quality' of paper being issued is declining. We are seeing a structural shift where the 'public' is essentially funding the 'private' sector's mistakes at peak valuations.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, as someone at Zerodha, are you seeing retail investors getting 'IPO fatigue,' or is the FOMO still overriding the data on muted returns?

source_articles.title: Record IPOs, muted returns: The other side of India’s primary market boom
Nomura Slashes Nifty Target: 24,900 by Dec 2026
Impact Analysis of RBIs Revised Capital Market Exposure Directions

themes.hook: 70% of Indian AI startups are just expensive 'wrappers' for ChatGPT. Google and Accel just called the bluff.
key_data_points: ~70% of applicants for the Google/Accel Atoms AI accelerator were rejected as 'AI wrappers'.
LTIMindtree is partnering with IIT Kharagpur to upskill its workforce specifically in AI.
India's agentic AI startups are facing a 'funding test' as they move from pilot to proof.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'wrapper' era is dead, but the labor arbitrage era is just getting a software update. The real value in Indian AI isn't in building the next LLM; it's the massive structural re-tooling of the IT services workforce. We are moving from 'coding for hire' to 'prompt engineering and agent management' at scale.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, do you think Indian IT firms can actually transition to AI-first models, or will the 'per-seat' billing model collapse before they can upskill?

source_articles.title: 70% of rejected applicants were AI wrappers
LTIMindtree partners with IIT Kharagpur to upskill workforce
Pilot to proof: India's agentic AI startups face a funding test

themes.hook: Indian mid-caps are no longer just making spare parts for tractors; they are entering the global 'Death Economy' supply chain.
key_data_points: An Indian auto component maker has officially joined the NATO supply chain.
Goodluck India has started its first-ever defense export shipment.
The US is investigating 16 economies, including India, under Section 301 for trade barriers.
themes.non_obvious_angle: While everyone watches 'China Plus One' in electronics, the real structural shift is in high-precision engineering for defense. Defense exports are the ultimate 'quality' stamp; if you can build for NATO, you can build for anyone. This creates a massive re-rating catalyst for boring, legacy engineering firms.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, is the 'Defense' theme in India overhyped at current P/E levels, or is the export potential actually large enough to justify the valuations?

source_articles.title: From tractors to tanks: This auto component maker joins NATO supply chain
Goodluck India Starts First Defence Export Shipment
Why is Trump targeting 16 economies, including India?
